# Fix: area_code Must Be Manual, Not Auto-Generated

## Problem

Area codes come from architectural plans (the architect assigns them). They are NOT auto-generated by the system. The current implementation auto-generates codes like `2-A-K-1` which don't match the building plans. Also, the same code CAN appear on multiple floors (e.g., all Master Baths might be "B.1.2.2" on every floor).

## Changes

### 1. Remove auto-generation of area_code

- Remove the code pattern selector from the onboarding wizard (the dropdown with `auto` / `flat` / `none` options)
- Remove any logic that auto-generates area_code on area creation
- area_code starts as NULL for all areas
- area_code is filled either via CSV import or manual edit

### 2. area_code is NOT unique

```sql
-- area_code should NOT have a unique constraint
-- Multiple areas can have the same code (same plan code on different floors)
-- The unique identifier is always the UUID id
-- area_code is just a display label from the building plans
```

If there's a unique index on area_code, DROP IT:
```sql
DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_areas_code_unique;
-- Keep the non-unique index for search:
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_areas_code ON areas(area_code);
```

### 3. Onboarding Quick Add — remove code generation, add optional code input

In the Quick Add step, remove the "Area code pattern" section entirely. Instead, add a note:

```tsx
<p className="text-xs text-muted mt-4">
  Area codes from your building plans can be added later in Settings → Areas, 
  or imported via CSV.
</p>
```

### 4. CSV import — area_code is optional column

The CSV import already supports an optional `code` column. Keep it as-is. When present, the code is saved. When absent, area_code stays NULL.

```csv
floor,unit,type,code,description
24,A,Bathroom,B.1.2.2,Master Bath - Marble
24,A,Bathroom,,Hall Bath - Standard
24,A,Kitchen,K.1.2.1,Full kitchen
```

Row 2 has no code — that's fine. area_code = NULL.

### 5. Settings → Areas — editable area_code field

In Settings (or in a new "Areas" management page), each area should have an editable code field:

```tsx
// In the areas management table/list
<td>
  <input
    type="text"
    value={area.area_code || ''}
    onChange={e => updateAreaCode(area.id, e.target.value || null)}
    placeholder="From plans"
    className="font-mono text-xs w-28 bg-transparent border-b border-white/10 
               focus:border-amber-500 outline-none px-1 py-0.5"
  />
</td>
```

### 6. Display — always show code + context to disambiguate duplicates

When displaying an area that has an area_code, always include floor + unit context so duplicate codes are distinguishable:

**In the Ready Board grid (area row):**
```tsx
<td className="pl-16">
  {area.area_code && (
    <span className="font-mono text-[11px] text-white/40 mr-2">
      {area.area_code}
    </span>
  )}
  <span className="font-medium">{area.name}</span>
  {area.description && (
    <span className="text-xs text-white/30 ml-2">· {area.description}</span>
  )}
</td>
```

The grid already shows the area under its Floor → Unit group, so the context is implicit. No need to repeat "Floor 24, Unit A" in the cell.

**In search results or flat lists (where context is NOT implicit):**
```tsx
<div>
  {area.area_code && (
    <span className="font-mono text-xs text-muted mr-2">{area.area_code}</span>
  )}
  <span className="font-medium">{area.name}</span>
  <span className="text-xs text-muted ml-2">
    · Floor {area.floor_number} · Unit {area.unit_name}
  </span>
</div>
```

**In the foreman mobile app:**
The area card already shows under its Unit header, so floor + unit context is visible. Just show the code badge if it exists:

```tsx
{area.area_code ? (
  <Text style={{ fontFamily: 'monospace', fontSize: 11, color: '#475569',
    backgroundColor: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.05)', paddingHorizontal: 6,
    paddingVertical: 2, borderRadius: 4 }}>
    {area.area_code}
  </Text>
) : null}
```

**In the detail panel (when clicking a cell):**
```tsx
<div>
  <div className="text-xs text-muted">
    Floor {floor.number} · Unit {unit.name}
  </div>
  <div className="flex items-center gap-2 mt-1">
    {area.area_code && (
      <span className="font-mono text-sm text-blue-400 bg-blue-500/10 px-2 py-0.5 rounded">
        {area.area_code}
      </span>
    )}
    <span className="text-lg font-bold">{area.name}</span>
  </div>
  {area.description && (
    <div className="text-sm text-muted mt-1">{area.description}</div>
  )}
</div>
```

### 7. Update existing seed data

For the demo account (383 Madison), clear all auto-generated codes:

```sql
UPDATE areas SET area_code = NULL WHERE project_id = (
  SELECT id FROM projects WHERE name = '383 Madison Avenue'
);
```

Or better: seed a few realistic codes for the demo areas that have delays (so the demo looks real):

```sql
-- Only set codes on the areas that are visible in the demo (floors with activity)
UPDATE areas SET area_code = 'B.1.2.2' WHERE name = 'Master Bath' AND floor_number = 24 AND unit_name = '24A';
UPDATE areas SET area_code = 'B.1.2.3' WHERE name = 'Hall Bath' AND floor_number = 24 AND unit_name = '24A';
UPDATE areas SET area_code = 'K.1.2.1' WHERE name = 'Kitchen' AND floor_number = 24 AND unit_name = '24A';
-- Most areas can stay NULL — realistic because most PMs don't enter codes for every area
```

### Summary

- area_code is MANUAL, never auto-generated
- area_code is NOT unique (duplicates allowed across floors)
- area_code is OPTIONAL (NULL is valid)
- Remove code pattern selector from onboarding
- CSV import fills codes when present
- Settings page allows manual code editing
- Display always includes floor + unit context when showing codes in flat lists
- Foreman mobile shows code badge only when code exists
